name: Course Schedule I
statement: There are a total of N tasks, labeled from 0 to N-1. Given an array arr where arr[i] = [a, b] indicates that you must take course b first if you want to take course a. Find if it is possible to finish all tasks.
constraints:
•   1 <= N <= 2000
  •   0 <= arr.length <= 5000
  •   arr[i].length == 2
  •   0 <= arr[i][0], arr[i][1] < N
  •   All the pairs arr[i] are unique.

examples.explanation: It is possible to finish all the tasks in the order : 0 1 2 3. First, we will finish task 0. Then we will finish task 1, task 2, and task 3.

It is impossible to finish all the tasks. Let’s analyze the pairs: For pair {0, 1} -> we need to finish task 1 first and then task 0. (order : 1 0). For pair{3, 2} -> we need to finish task 2 first and then task 3. (order: 2 3). For pair {1, 3} -> we need to finish task 3 first and then task 1. (order: 3 1). But for pair {3, 0} -> we need to finish task 0 first and then task 3 but task 0 requires task 1 and task 1 requires task 3. So, it is not possible to finish all the tasks.
